que: What is the primary purpose of the Center widget in Flutter?
options: To align its child to the top-left
To center its child both vertically and horizontally within its parent
To expand its child to fill all available space

que: Center is commonly used to do what?
options: Place a widget in the middle of the available space
Place a widget at the bottom of the screen

que: In the example, which widget is centered?
Which parameter of Center holds the widget to be centered?
According to the file, is the child parameter conceptually required?
options: Yes, it is the widget to center
No, Center cannot have a child
Child must be a Text widget

que: Which parameter scales the width of the Center based on the child’s width?
Which parameter scales the height of the Center based on the child’s height?
What happens if widthFactor and heightFactor are null?
options: Center sizes itself to its child
Center tries to be as big as possible in the given constraints

que: Which constructor signature matches the Center widget described?
options: Center({Key? key, double? widthFactor, double? heightFactor, Widget? child})
Center({double width, double height, Widget child})

que: According to the quick tips, what is a suggested use of Center?
options: To quickly center any widget in its parent
To create a list of widgets

que: Which layout widgets are mentioned to combine with Center for more complex layouts?
Which is listed as something to avoid with Center?
options: Nesting multiple Centers in the same layout region

que: If you need custom alignment other than exact center, what widget is recommended instead of Center?
Why should you avoid nesting multiple Centers?
options: It has no effect and can make the layout harder to read

que: Which parameter is NOT listed as a main parameter of Center in the file?
What does widthFactor do when set to 2.0 (conceptually)?
options: Makes Center take half the child’s width
Makes Center ignore the child’s width
Makes Center’s width equal to twice the child’s width

que: What does heightFactor do when set to 0.5 (conceptually)?
options: Center’s height is half of the child’s height
Center’s height is double the child’s height
Center expands to full screen height

que: Which import is typically required to use Center in a Flutter app?
Which statement best describes Center’s alignment behavior?
Which of the following best summarizes when NOT to use Center?
options: When you need custom alignment like top-left or bottom-right
When you have only one child
